mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declines in unemployment (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 100k and UNRATE decreases > 0.5pp in 1 month
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or individual series show no predictive power in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 900k signals strong consumer demand, driving equity valuations higher through improved corporate revenue prospects over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods when HOUST exceeds 900k, or the series shows no significant correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to higher real interest rates and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the series shows no significant negative correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) above 100 signals stronger manufacturing activity, improving corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods when INDPRO exceeds 100, or the series shows no significant positive correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates late-cycle demand momentum, driving S&P 500 returns higher over 60 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: The combined effect of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or individual series show no predictive power in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) paired with low unemployment (UNRATE) signal a strong economy, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or individual series show no predictive power in isolation.

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer spending (RSAFS) above 450 billion signals robust demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods when RSAFS exceeds 450k, or the series shows no significant positive correlation with SPX returns.
